name: usebrick corpus (LOCAL TEMPLATE)
description: Template for corpus-manifest.local.json. Copy this file to corpus-manifest.local.json and fill in the location-specific values. The local file is gitignored because the absolute path + size estimates + repo list are private to each maintainer's machine. The contract surface (this template) is committed so the format is documented and stable.
instructions: 2. Fill in the 'location.path' with your absolute path to the corpus
4. Update 'latestCalibration' with the current v# run
5. List the categories you have on disk in 'structure.negative.categories'
7. Update 'sizeEstimate' to match your local totals

negative.description: Human-written code samples, organized by category.

filelists.description: Pre-computed file lists for each negative/positive source.

scan.description: Scan output dirs (symlinks to source files for the calibration pipeline).

labeled.description: Manually labeled subsets. Empty until curation work is done.

notes: The corpus is intentionally OUTSIDE the git repo. It is too large to version, and a shallow clone is faster than a checkout for the calibration pipeline.
The 'location' field uses an absolute path. To move the corpus, update corpus-manifest.local.json (and the symlinks inside v5/scan and v7/scan).

The corpus is not a contract in the JSON-Schema sense — slopbrick reads source files from it but doesn't validate against a schema. The 'contract' field documents the related (but separate) types/schemas that the calibration output must conform to.
